Geranium douglasii,  also known as the Douglas's cranesbill, is a flowering plant in the genus Geranium. 

Geranium douglasii has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. 

The bioactive constituents of Geranium douglasii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Geranium douglasii include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and alleviate digestive issues.
